feedback.text: I'd like to add video games as a whole as another issue that this act should tackle, besides the gambling-like design that some video games have. More precisely, the fact that older games, especially those with a mandatory internet connection, are often abandoned after a few years and are unplayable by customers who bought them. This not only hurts preservation, but is also anti-consumer since that even if a consumer has a physical copy of a game, it may become unusable by the whim of a publisher or developer.
